text: The realization of Pb-free relaxors with high capacitive energy density (W rec ) and high energy storage efficiency (η) is still a great challenge to the development of advanced pulsed power systems. Even though local compositional heterogeneity is necessary for generating complex polar structure in solid solution relaxors, studies on its role on energy storage properties are limited. In this work, we for the first time identified that designing local chemical clustering can lead to high energy storage efficiency and large energy density in relaxor materials. The values of W rec and η for a designed Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 TiO 3 -BaTiO 2 -BaTiO 3 relaxor composition were measured as high as 15.2 J cm -3 and 91%, respectively. Combining the information derived from three-dimensional atomistic model constructed by neutron/X-ray total scattering and the reverse Monte Carlo method, the existence of subnanometer scale clustering of Bi, Na and Ba is evidenced, which have different polar displacements as confirmed by density functional theory calculations. All these lead to the realization of polar structure with small polar clusters and strong length and direction fluctuations in the unit-cell polar vectors, thus demonstrating the improved high-field polarizability, reduced hysteresis and high breakdown strength. In addition, the favorable polar structure features in the relaxor material also demonstrated a unique field-increased η, excellent stability and discharge capacity.
explanation: The text resembles a polished scientific abstract with domain-specific details, but a few awkward phrases and minor typographical inconsistencies (e.g., 'BaTiO 2 -BaTiO 3', 'for the first time identified') hint at possible AI involvement or editing artifacts.
